experiments.title: Exact scaling exponent table
experiments.hypothesis: The recorded velocity, vorticity, pressure, and force exponents follow from one exact scaling rule.
invariants: Space dimension equals 3
Parabolic time power equals 2
experiments.method: Rational arithmetic in navier_lab.scaling.
experiments.certificate: Unit-test assertions are the algebraic certificate.
experiments.success_criterion: All canonical critical and energy exponents equal their exact rational targets.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The dossier or implementation has a scaling error.

experiments.title: 2607 logarithmic exponent bookkeeping
experiments.hypothesis: The paper's stated distribution and rearrangement exponents transform algebraically as claimed.
invariants: This checks exponent arithmetic only
It does not prove O'Neil or energy inequalities
experiments.method: Exact Fraction transformations in navier_lab.log_chain.
experiments.certificate: Unit-test assertions and command-line report.
experiments.success_criterion: Vorticity tail (3/2,3/2) maps to rearrangement (2/3,1) and velocity tail (3,3).
experiments.failure_interpretation: At least one claimed exponent conversion is algebraically inconsistent.

experiments.title: Pinned arXiv 2607.08866v2 source cache
experiments.hypothesis: The exact TeX source can be reproducibly fetched, hashed, and safely extracted.
invariants: Version v2
No tracked copy without redistribution permission
No unsafe archive paths
experiments.method: navier_lab.source_cache fetches the arXiv e-print and writes a manifest.
experiments.certificate: SHA-256 manifest plus archive member list.
experiments.success_criterion: The manifest names chaos_sphere.tex and records a nonempty SHA-256 digest.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The source version is not pinned or the archive layout changed.

experiments.title: Source-anchored audit of 2607 foundation obligations
experiments.hypothesis: The exact v2 source permits a bounded verdict on O2607-01 through O2607-04 without assuming any downstream commutator estimate.
invariants: Pinned TeX SHA-256
Every source display and imported theorem is line-anchored
No downstream theorem conclusion is assumed
experiments.method: Parse the complete TeX source, rewrite all foundation quantifiers, track constant dependencies, and independently derive the tensor cancellation.
experiments.certificate: The proof map and its linked analytic counterexamples and tensor derivation.
experiments.success_criterion: Each of O2607-01 through O2607-04 receives a verified, repaired, or unsupported verdict with exact scope.
experiments.failure_interpretation: At least one foundation quantifier, dependency, or tensor identity remains unresolved.

experiments.title: Weak-L(3/2) multi-core localization counterexample
experiments.hypothesis: A uniform weak-L(3/2) bound alone does not confine every high-level set to one ball of radius proportional to lambda^(-1/2).
invariants: Divergence-free realization is not needed for the first measure-theoretic counterexample
A later PDE-consistent version is separate
experiments.method: Construct separated critical spikes with controlled distribution function and unbounded covering diameter.
experiments.certificate: A written proof and executable distribution/covering check.
experiments.success_criterion: The example has bounded weak-L(3/2) quasi-norm while violating single-ball containment uniformly.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The proposed spike weights or geometry do not preserve the critical bound.

experiments.title: Weak-L(3/2) one-core anisotropic localization counterexample
experiments.hypothesis: A uniform weak-L(3/2) bound does not force even one connected point-centered high-level set into a ball of radius proportional to lambda^(-1/2).
invariants: The distribution exponent remains exactly 3/2
The example is scalar and makes no PDE-consistency claim
experiments.method: Use anisotropic boxes with half-width powers 1/4, 5/8, and 5/8.
experiments.certificate: A written proof and exact rational/integer exponent checks.
experiments.success_criterion: The scaled distribution tail is constant while the necessary critical-radius covering constant grows as lambda^(1/4).
experiments.failure_interpretation: The anisotropic volume or diameter exponents were calculated incorrectly.

experiments.title: Logarithmic covering entropy in the truncated-energy step
experiments.hypothesis: A countable high-vorticity cover replaces single-ball localization whenever its aggregated local weak-L(3/2) gain vanishes uniformly, and the resulting ell-(3/2) ledger is sharp from the local bounds alone.
invariants: The localized commutator constant is uniform in centre, radius, and time
Weak-L(3/2) distribution functions aggregate with exponent 3/2
A measurable disjoint refinement removes overlap multiplicity
No covering geometry is claimed to follow from Navier-Stokes dynamics
experiments.method: Define the logarithmic Hausdorff-content ledger of a ball cover, aggregate the local commutator bounds by distribution functions, and propagate the resulting coefficient through quadratic absorption, interpolation, Young, damping, and the vorticity-tail estimate.
experiments.certificate: A written covering lemma with a sharpness example and exact exponent checks for equal-radius and polynomial-radius covers.
experiments.success_criterion: The proof identifies the exact cover cost, the weakest uniform vanishing condition for absorption, and the critical equal-radius core-count threshold.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The proposed entropy does not control the restricted weak-L(3/2) norm or its energy-step exponent bookkeeping is incorrect.

experiments.title: Divergence-free packet obstruction to perimeter-controlled cover entropy
experiments.hypothesis: Kinetic energy, critical L3 velocity and L(3/2) vorticity size, and even vanishing perimeter of a high-vorticity set do not by themselves force its logarithmic cover content to vanish.
invariants: Every velocity field is smooth, compactly supported, and divergence-free
Vorticity is the exact curl and is Biot-Savart consistent
The critical L3 velocity norm vanishes
The strong L(3/2) vorticity norm is uniform
Kinetic energy and high-level perimeter vanish along the family
The family is not asserted to be one Navier-Stokes evolution
experiments.method: Scale and widely separate identical compactly supported vortex packets, choose the packet count at the logarithmic threshold, and prove a matching lower bound for every admissible ball cover.
experiments.certificate: A written construction and exact rational exponent checks for volume, perimeter, kinetic energy, both critical norms, and cover entropy.
experiments.success_criterion: At levels tending to infinity, the logarithmic cover content stays bounded below while the listed PDE-kinematic quantities remain bounded or vanish.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The packet field is not divergence-free and Biot-Savart consistent, a claimed scaling exponent is wrong, or a cheaper admissible cover defeats the entropy lower bound.

experiments.title: Componentwise viscous decay of isolated vortex packets
experiments.hypothesis: Under the repaired local commutator estimate, any number of disjoint full-vorticity packets supported in uniformly shrinking balls are diffusion-dominated without paying a packet-count entropy.
invariants: The starting identity is the exact smooth vorticity enstrophy balance
Each packet is an H01 component of the full vorticity, not merely a high-level excess
Lorentz Holder inequality and Sobolev are applied separately on each packet
The packet count must cancel from quadratic stretching absorption
No conclusion is claimed for packets connected by a low-vorticity background
experiments.method: Split enstrophy and palinstrophy over disjoint zero-boundary packets, insert the centre-uniform local weak-L(3/2) commutator estimate componentwise, apply Lorentz-Sobolev and Poincare, and compare the resulting viscous and nonlinear times.
experiments.certificate: A written decay lemma with a strain threshold and exact exponent checks for lifetime, self-strain, diffusion, and packet count.
experiments.success_criterion: The proof gives count-free absorption, lifetime O(r^2/nu), and an order-nu lower threshold for the local weak-L(3/2) strain needed to prevent decay.
experiments.failure_interpretation: A hidden component-count factor enters the energy sum, the local strain threshold has the wrong scale, or the zero-boundary hypothesis is insufficient for the Sobolev-Poincare steps.

experiments.title: Exact logarithmic threshold for the sparse-analyticity endgame
experiments.hypothesis: Every positive logarithmic improvement in the critical vorticity distribution produces a sparse velocity scale strictly below the analytic radius, while the zero-log endpoint remains constant-sensitive.
invariants: The vorticity distribution exponent is fixed at the critical value 3/2
The logarithmic exponent gamma is propagated symbolically
O'Neil transfer preserves the rearrangement logarithmic exponent
The bounded velocity-background repair remains an input
No claim is made that arbitrary Clay data generate a positive gamma
experiments.method: Invert the general vorticity tail, transfer it through the order-one Riesz potential, invert the velocity rearrangement, derive the uniform sparse radius, and compare it with the restarted analytic radius.
experiments.certificate: A written cutoff-aware exponent proof and exact Fraction tests for general gamma, including gamma equal to zero and one.
experiments.success_criterion: The proof states the exact gamma threshold and decides whether the one-log tail from componentwise packet control closes the downstream endgame.
experiments.failure_interpretation: A logarithmic exponent changes incorrectly under inversion or O'Neil transfer, or the sparse radius fails to fit inside the analytic radius at the claimed threshold.

experiments.title: Mixed global-local Lorentz control of the component linear source
experiments.hypothesis: Combining the global weak-L(3/2) strain bound with the local logarithmic commutator gain restores a positive linear-source log gain for every count of comparable critical components, including the cubic-log endpoint.
invariants: The global strain bound follows from Calderon-Zygmund control of vorticity
Component supports are disjoint and have one comparable radius
Both the global and local distribution bounds are used at every amplitude
The proof estimates an l2 sum of L(6/5,2) component norms
No conclusion is claimed for arbitrary variable-radius or connected geometry
experiments.method: Write the L(6/5,2) norm by distribution functions, bound the sum of 5/3 powers by a local maximum times the global distribution, split at the local support crossover, and insert the result into dual Lorentz-Sobolev and Young.
experiments.certificate: A written mixed-norm lemma with exact exponent optimisation and Fraction tests for the local, mixed, cubic-endpoint, and worst-count regimes.
experiments.success_criterion: The cubic-log endpoint recovers a positive vorticity-tail exponent, and the better of the local-only and mixed estimates retains at least one logarithm for every comparable critical packet count.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The distribution square-sum has an untracked count factor, the Lorentz dual exponent is wrong, or optimisation leaves a packet count with zero logarithmic gain.

experiments.title: Total-volume aggregation and the vanishing-tail endgame
experiments.hypothesis: A uniform o(1) local weak-L(3/2) strain bound on disjoint zero-boundary high-level components, together with global weak-L(3/2) vorticity, makes component count and radius scales irrelevant: total superlevel volume yields an o(lambda^(-3/2)) vorticity tail, and any such vanishing critical-tail improvement closes the sparse-analyticity endgame.
invariants: The components are disjoint and their zero extensions support componentwise Sobolev estimates
Global weak-L(3/2) vorticity bounds the total superlevel volume
The local weak-L(3/2) strain norm tends to zero uniformly over components
No quantitative decay rate or comparable-radius assumption is inserted
The little-o rearrangement transfer is uniform on the terminal interval
The result remains conditional and does not establish a Clay alternative
experiments.method: Apply the mixed distribution square-sum with the total superlevel volume as the common support cap, close the truncated-energy inequality with the resulting vanishing factor, and prove directly through O'Neil rearrangement that weak critical little-o vorticity transfers to weak critical little-o velocity.
experiments.certificate: A written total-volume lemma and little-o endgame proof, with exact Fraction checks for every algebraic exponent in the component-to-vorticity-to-velocity-to-radius chain.
experiments.success_criterion: The component residual ledger is no longer needed: uniform vanishing of the maximum containing radius supplies enough local strain decay for arbitrary component counts and radius distributions.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The total-volume support cap creates a hidden count or scale factor, the energy conversion loses the vanishing factor, or O'Neil transfer does not preserve critical little-o decay.

experiments.title: Critical-scale smooth localization of truncated stretching
experiments.hypothesis: A finite-overlap square partition at radius R proportional to lambda^(-1/2), together with vanishing of the high-level local weak-L(3/2) positive aligned strain norm and the global weak strain distribution, absorbs every quadratic and linear cutoff term without any component geometry; the repaired logarithmic direction hypothesis supplies one quantitative instance.
invariants: The smooth partition satisfies sum eta_k squared equals one exactly
Partition overlap and scaled gradient constants are independent of level
The global weak strain distribution is charged only through one square-sum
Only positive aligned strain restricted to the high-vorticity superlevel set enters the local norm; negative aligned strain is favourable
Every IMS term involving R^(-2) times truncated energy is retained
The radius is selected only after both quadratic and linear ledgers are derived
The theorem remains conditional on weak-vorticity, high-level local positive-strain decay, and velocity-background hypotheses; the repaired direction extension is sufficient but not necessary
experiments.method: Construct a normalized lattice partition, prove an overlap version of the mixed L(6/5,2) distribution square-sum, use the exact IMS identity for the quadratic term, and use Lorentz-Sobolev plus Cauchy and damping for both pieces of the linear term.
experiments.certificate: A written partition theorem with every cutoff term displayed and exact Fraction tests for the radius optimization, source power, logarithmic gain, and resulting vorticity tail.
experiments.success_criterion: At R proportional to lambda^(-1/2), diffusion and support-sensitive damping absorb all partition errors while the residual is O(lambda^(3/2) times the square root of the high-level local positive-strain norm); the repaired logarithmic commutator gives the quantitative log(lambda)^(-1/2) case and eliminates every ROUTE-R3A component or covering hypothesis.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The overlap distribution sum has a hidden count, the IMS identity leaves an uncontrolled cross term, or one linear cutoff residual is critical with no vanishing gain.

experiments.title: Zero-set-safe truncated direction and critical commutator defect
experiments.hypothesis: A level-dependent direction that vanishes below delta times lambda and equals the true direction above lambda splits high-level stretching into one truncated-direction commutator and a low-vorticity singular-integral remainder; the remainder is uniformly O(delta times one plus log one over delta) on critical balls, while an exact Navier-Stokes scaling family tests whether the commutator can vanish without cross-scale dynamics.
invariants: The truncated direction is defined as zero at every vorticity zero
The identity is exact on the high-vorticity superlevel set
Only the positive aligned-strain part is an obstruction in the truncated-enstrophy inequality
A fixed failure size permits one fixed truncation parameter for all dynamic rescalings
The low-vorticity remainder amplitude is at most a fixed multiple of delta times lambda
Near-field and every far annulus are retained in the remainder estimate
The critical scaling family has uniform weak-L(3/2) vorticity and solves unforced Navier-Stokes locally
The scaling family is not one blow-up trajectory and does not establish a Clay alternative
experiments.method: Construct an amplitude-adapted cutoff, expand the strain Calderon-Zygmund identity using commutators, estimate the low remainder by L2 near-field control and weak-L(3/2) annular mass bounds, then scale an explicit compactly supported divergence-free velocity with constant core vorticity and nonzero aligned strain.
experiments.certificate: A written exact identity, optimized annular estimate, and smooth PDE-consistent saturation family, with exact Fraction tests for critical scaling and the delta remainder ledger.
experiments.success_criterion: Either the truncated commutator is proved to vanish and ROUTE-R3B advances to regularity, or all automatic zero-set and low-level effects are removed and a nonvanishing scale-critical commutator is isolated by an exact PDE-consistent family.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The cutoff identity has an omitted term, the far-field remainder fails to vanish with delta, or the proposed scaling field is not divergence-free, does not have nonzero aligned strain, or loses critical weak-vorticity control.

experiments.title: Ancient rescaling and the commutator concentration defect
experiments.hypothesis: A nonvanishing truncated commutator along one putative blow-up rescales to backward-ancient time domains with invariant critical bounds, but the endpoint hypotheses may yield only a nonzero concentration measure rather than a nontrivial ancient function unless critical strong compactness is proved.
invariants: The parabolic rescaling normalizes the vorticity level and critical ball simultaneously
The backward rescaled time interval tends to minus infinity
Weak-L(3/2) vorticity and weak-L3 velocity norms are scale invariant
Calderon-Zygmund far-field tails vanish uniformly on compact sets
A selected distribution witness produces a bounded nonzero Radon measure
No ancient solution or preserved commutator is claimed from weak or subcritical convergence alone
experiments.method: Rescale a failure sequence, inventory all invariant bounds and time domains, derive uniform singular-integral tail tightness, state a strong critical convergence certificate for passing the high-level commutator, and construct a witness-threshold concentration measure when that certificate fails.
experiments.certificate: A written compactness-gate theorem and defect-measure construction, with exact Fraction tests for Navier-Stokes scaling, time expansion, weak-norm invariance, witness mass, and subcritical loss.
experiments.success_criterion: The precise assumptions needed for a nontrivial ancient commutator profile are separated from what the current endpoint hypotheses actually provide, and any missing critical mass is represented by a nonzero normalized defect measure rather than silently discarded.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The rescaled time interval is not ancient, nonlocal tails are not tight, the witness measure is unbounded or can vanish, or the stated strong topology does not pass the thresholded commutator.

experiments.title: Atomic commutator defects and secondary ancient rescaling
experiments.hypothesis: Failure of the exact positive aligned-strain target yields a genuinely positive commutator witness; the endpoint critical bounds then produce an ancient distributional velocity limit at every defect scale, while an atomic witness has a canonical fixed-mass secondary rescaling whose exact amplitude, vorticity threshold, time domain, and measure reveal either a function-density bubble or unresolved natural-scale dust.
invariants: Local W(1,q) velocity compactness uses only 6/5 less than q less than 3/2
The quadratic Navier-Stokes term passes by strong local spacetime L2 convergence
No suitable-solution or local-energy conclusion is inferred from distributional compactness
Witness sets can be pruned so both aligned strain and the truncated commutator are positive
The secondary parabolic rescaling preserves critical weak norms and defect mass
The normalized vorticity threshold becomes rho squared and is not silently reset to one
A fixed atomic mass forces the dimensionless commutator witness level away from zero
A bounded witness level yields a nonzero positive commutator profile, while an unbounded level retains a measure defect
Backward persistence is claimed only under an explicit scale-adapted temporal modulus
experiments.method: Apply local Lorentz embeddings and Aubin-Lions compactness to extract an ancient distributional solution, decompose the witness measure into absolutely continuous, singular-continuous, and atomic parts, select an atomic concentration radius, carry out the exact secondary Navier-Stokes scaling, and split the result by dimensionless witness level and natural-scale granularity.
experiments.certificate: A written compactness and concentration theorem with an exact half-level temporal persistence inequality, plus Fraction tests for the Sobolev interval, secondary thresholds, critical measure Jacobian, subcritical loss, natural scale, and parabolic clock.
experiments.success_criterion: Every time-zero defect is classified as atomless, a coherent atomic density bubble, a unit-level child bubble, or a certified dust cloud; the ancient distributional equation is passed rigorously and the remaining temporal and suitability hypotheses are explicit.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The endpoint spaces do not compactly pass the quadratic equation, secondary scaling changes the defect mass, an atomic witness can lose all mass after normalization, or backward persistence is asserted without a scale-uniform temporal estimate.

experiments.title: Natural commutator clock and atomic dust obstruction
experiments.hypothesis: A positive witness of height sigma has natural length sigma^(-1/2) and natural time sigma^(-1), so moving-centre persistence is governed by a scale-invariant material commutator variation; bare order-zero commutator structure may still admit a fixed-mass atom composed of vanishing natural-scale dust.
invariants: One fixed low-vorticity truncation suffices after the failure size is fixed
The natural time divided by the atomic parabolic time is exactly theta^(-1)
The persistence certificate permits translation of the concentration centre
The time-integrated weak-L(3/2) material commutator variation is scale invariant
The differentiated proxy retains diffusion, nonlinear flux, threshold crossing, direction variation, and amplitude variation
The dust multiplier has uniformly bounded BMO norm
The dust source has uniformly bounded strong L(3/2) norm
The witness measures converge to an atom while every natural-scale ball has vanishing mass
The dust construction is not asserted to arise from Navier-Stokes vorticity
experiments.method: Fix the truncation at the failure tolerance, normalize time by the witness height, pull a smooth threshold-safe commutator proxy back along moving centre paths, differentiate it through the vorticity equation, and construct a separated lattice of scaled Riesz-commutator cells whose self-interactions dominate their logarithmic cross-interactions.
experiments.certificate: A written moving-centre persistence-or-variation theorem and explicit Riesz dust construction, with Fraction tests for cell, natural, cloud, amplitude, witness-mass, clock, interaction, and spacetime-variation powers.
experiments.success_criterion: The correct natural clock and exact PDE variation terms are fixed, and either abstract commutator structure yields a no-dust lower bound or a bounded-BMO strong-critical counterexample proves that any no-dust theorem must use Navier-Stokes coupling.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The natural-time normalization is not scale invariant, the moving-centre level-set argument loses witness mass without variation, cross interactions overwhelm the copied self-commutators, or a natural-scale ball retains fixed positive dust mass.

experiments.title: Natural-clock heat ledger and parabolic stress cascade
experiments.hypothesis: Writing the vorticity equation with the critical velocity stress avoids the formal weak-L1 flux, but viscosity spends exactly two derivatives on each high shell; time coherence may recover an order-zero multiplier, whereas endpoint spatial size alone may permit a local time-frequency cascade with a nonzero terminal trace.
invariants: Velocity stress and vorticity are both critical in weak L(3/2)
The order-two stress map and one parabolic shell time have net frequency power zero
Low shells have a summable dyadic-square weight and high shells have no decay
A time-independent stress recombines into one uniformly bounded order-zero multiplier
The forced cascade lies in one normalized natural ball and activates only one shell at each time
The forced cascade stress tends to zero even in strong L-infinity-time L(3/2)-space
The terminal weak-L(3/2) response remains bounded below
The forced tensor is not asserted to be a Navier-Stokes velocity stress
A naive unit-velocity realisation of the forced carriers violates the vorticity endpoint by J^(1/3)
experiments.method: Rewrite the vorticity Duhamel term as an order-two heat operator applied to u tensor u, integrate each dyadic shell over the natural clock, separate the coherent terminal stress from its temporal oscillation, and pack smooth modulated stress cells with disjoint parabolic time windows inside one fixed ball.
experiments.certificate: A written dyadic multiplier estimate, coherent-stress cancellation, local smooth forced-parabolic cascade, explicit Navier-Stokes scope boundary, and Fraction tests for critical scaling, heat cancellation, cell packing, sequence aggregation, and the failed naive velocity realisation.
experiments.success_criterion: The weak-L1 dead end and any automatic high-frequency heat gain are resolved, and either endpoint stress control yields a terminal modulus or an explicit cascade isolates the additional same-solution Navier-Stokes input required.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The stress representation is not endpoint bounded, the coherent multiplier is not order zero, the cascade time windows overlap, its terminal weak norm vanishes, or the example is mistakenly promoted to a Navier-Stokes evolution.

experiments.title: Same-solution stress gain and natural-band child granularity
experiments.hypothesis: Because the stress is generated by the same velocity whose curl has a critical weak-L(3/2) bound, one velocity shell gains K^(-1/2) in weak L2; the full stress shell then gains K^(-1/2) in normable weak L(6/5), forcing every fixed-height positive-stretching witness into finitely many natural-band balls after one natural time.
invariants: The theorem assumes the repaired uniform weak-L(3/2) vorticity hypothesis and does not derive it for Clay data
Biot-Savart and Lorentz-Bernstein give a K^(-1/2) weak-L2 velocity-shell gain
Weak L2 times weak L3 lies in normable weak L(6/5)
Low-high and high-high paraproducts retain the same K^(-1/2) stress-shell gain
A high-high input tail also decays by the square root of its minimum frequency
The initial high-frequency heat trace decays exponentially after one natural time
The nonlinear high-frequency witness content decays as M^(-3/5) with exact cancellation of the witness height
The natural-band superlevel set has a scale-invariant finite covering number
One natural ball retains fixed positive aligned-strain witness mass
The natural-ball selection precedes and excludes a purely atomless or singular-continuous terminal defect
The bounded-frequency terminal strain trace is nonzero and equicontinuous in natural time
No suitability or backward persistence of the aligned direction is inferred
experiments.method: Use Biot-Savart, Lorentz-Bernstein, Lorentz Holder, and Bony decomposition to prove the stress-shell gain; insert it into the mild vorticity equation, discard the small high-frequency strain set, cover the remaining bandlimited positive-stretching witness by a Lipschitz packing argument, and rescale the selected natural ball.
experiments.certificate: A written conditional theorem with stress paraproduct, mild high-tail, bandlimited covering, dust-exclusion, and nonzero-trace proofs, plus Fraction tests for all frequency, witness, covering, and time-modulus powers.
experiments.success_criterion: Same-solution coupling rules out the arbitrary stress cascade, high-high cancellation tail, atomless spatial dispersion, natural-scale dust, and zero ancient distributional limit under the repaired endpoint hypothesis, leaving only temporal direction and suitability gates.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The weak-L2 shell gain is unavailable, a paraproduct loses dyadic summability, the critical witness height does not cancel, the natural-band cover count depends on scale, or the selected terminal projection can still converge to zero.

experiments.title: Projective alignment evolution and viscous direction defect
experiments.hypothesis: The same finite-band strain rotates its vorticity direction favourably, while the only singular alignment term is viscous projective diffusion divided by vorticity magnitude; a smooth exact local Navier-Stokes shear family can retain this scale-invariant defect despite local energy equality and vanishing extra dissipation.
invariants: The projective equations are asserted only where vorticity is nonzero
The finite-band self-rotation term is twice a nonnegative Rayleigh variance
The projective diffusion content is invariant under three-dimensional Navier-Stokes scaling
The shear family solves unforced Navier-Stokes pointwise and satisfies local energy equality
One fixed-volume material set changes from alignment at most -7/25 to at least 7/25 in a vanishing fraction of one natural time
The shear contribution to scale-invariant dissipation tends to zero
The family has an unbounded linear background, infinite global energy, and no global endpoint vorticity bound
The countermodel is not Clay-admissible and does not kill ROUTE-R3B
The terminal projective Young measure is retained separately from the limiting vorticity magnitude
Suitability remains an independent unproved gate in the repaired conditional chain
experiments.method: Derive the material equations for vorticity direction, projective orientation, and finite-band Rayleigh alignment; isolate the scale-invariant viscous projective rate; verify an exact linear-strain two-shear Navier-Stokes solution whose slow and fast vorticity modes flip alignment; and pass the selected terminal directions to a projective Young measure.
experiments.certificate: A written tensor derivation, exact local Navier-Stokes substitution, fixed-measure sign-flip estimate, dissipation integral, critical scaling ledger, projective Young-measure reduction, and Fraction tests for the Rayleigh variance, mode equations, alignment constants, and defect powers.
experiments.success_criterion: Every nonprojective alignment term is classified, the local suitability shortcut is decisively rejected within its exact scope, and ROUTE-R3B is reduced to excluding zero-vorticity orientation mass by global Biot-Savart and one-trajectory structure or carrying its projective defect into suitable ancient rigidity.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The direction identity has a sign error, the shear model is not an exact Navier-Stokes solution, the flip set loses all measure, the projective content is not scale invariant, or the local countermodel is promoted beyond its non-Clay scope.

experiments.title: Zero-set obstruction to logarithmic-BMO vorticity direction
experiments.hypothesis: Smooth divergence-free vorticity need not admit a normalized direction with the bmo_(1/|log r|) modulus across its zero set.
invariants: The vorticity is smooth, compactly supported, and divergence-free
Changing values on the measure-zero zero axis cannot change mean oscillation
experiments.method: Take the curl of a compactly supported vector potential that yields (x1,-x2,0) near the origin and compute its scale-invariant directional oscillation.
experiments.certificate: The symmetry calculation gives mean zero and mean oscillation exactly one on every centered small ball.
experiments.success_criterion: The weighted-BMO seminorm diverges for every assignment on the zero axis.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The local direction average or divergence-free construction is incorrect.

experiments.title: Exact Biot-Savart unidirectional strain contraction
experiments.hypothesis: The unidirectional Biot-Savart contribution has zero Rayleigh contraction along the fixed vorticity direction, although its strain tensor need not vanish.
invariants: Levi-Civita convention epsilon_123 equals 1
The human index derivation is the proof and the finite grid is only a check
experiments.method: Differentiate Biot-Savart in indices, symmetrize, contract, and check the finite-dimensional tensor identity with exact integers.
experiments.certificate: A written principal-value derivation, a nonzero-tensor counterexample, and exact unit tests.
experiments.success_criterion: The Rayleigh numerator equals the scalar triple-product form and vanishes for every unidirectional source tested.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The tensor signs, index contractions, or stated cancellation are wrong.

experiments.title: Localized 2607 commutator reconstruction
experiments.hypothesis: The localized logarithmic commutator estimate at source L232-371 survives exact BMO extension, weak-Lorentz interpolation, and far-field reconstruction.
invariants: Pinned arXiv:2607.08866v2 TeX
Every operator is treated componentwise
No constant depends on radius or time
No downstream theorem conclusion is assumed
experiments.method: Match the Jones domain seminorm and ball geometry, interpolate one fixed CRW commutator from L(4/3) and L2, and recompute the exact kernel rearrangement and truncated dyadic shells.
experiments.certificate: The written source-anchored reconstruction with a constant ledger and explicit repairs.
experiments.success_criterion: O2607-05 through O2607-07 each receive an independently checkable verified, repaired, or unsupported verdict.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The preprint's localized logarithmic smoothing theorem is not available in its stated form.

experiments.title: Truncated 2607 vorticity-energy reconstruction
experiments.hypothesis: The source's positive-level vorticity test, Lorentz interpolation, Young absorption, damping, and terminal-interval threshold yield the claimed logarithmically improved high-vorticity tail.
invariants: Pinned arXiv:2607.08866v2 TeX
Testing is justified at vorticity zeros
One threshold is uniform on the stated terminal interval
No downstream rearrangement claim is assumed
experiments.method: Apply Kato regularisation, justify the positive-level test by space-time approximation, verify each Lorentz and Sobolev exponent, solve the damped scalar inequality, and retain explicit high-level cutoffs.
experiments.certificate: The written source-anchored energy derivation and constant ledger.
experiments.success_criterion: O2607-08 through O2607-10 each receive an independently checkable verified, repaired, or unsupported verdict.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The claimed logarithmic vorticity distribution gain is not established from the repaired localized commutator estimate.

experiments.title: Logarithmic 2607 rearrangement and velocity transfer
experiments.hypothesis: The repaired high-vorticity distribution tail can be inverted, transferred through the whole-space Biot-Savart kernel by O'Neil's theorem, and inverted again without losing its logarithmic exponent.
invariants: Pinned arXiv:2607.08866v2 TeX
Both inversions retain explicit cutoffs and inequality directions
The O'Neil integral is controlled on the whole volume axis
Velocity is distinguished from its Biot-Savart representative
experiments.method: Use explicit trial level and trial volume functions, compute the exact Riesz-kernel rearrangement, bound both O'Neil terms, and isolate the harmonic-background normalisation.
experiments.certificate: The written source-anchored inversion and convolution derivation with cutoff and normalisation ledgers.
experiments.success_criterion: O2607-11 and O2607-12 each receive an independently checkable verified, repaired, or unsupported verdict.

experiments.title: Sparse-analyticity and harmonic-measure endgame
experiments.hypothesis: The repaired velocity distribution tail can be synchronized with local spatial analyticity and a correctly posed slit-domain harmonic-measure argument to exclude the proposed first singular time.
invariants: Pinned arXiv:2607.08866v2 TeX
The evaluation time remains strictly below T-star
One sparse radius works around every spatial centre
Velocity harmonic background is explicitly normalized or uniformly bounded
The final contradiction occurs only after taking the spatial supremum
experiments.method: Choose an explicit sparse radius from the velocity tail, prove same-scale 3D-to-1D sparseness, evaluate at half a guaranteed local lifespan after an escape time, and apply the two-constants principle on the slit disk.
experiments.certificate: The source-anchored endgame reconstruction with exact geometric, temporal, harmonic-measure, and constant ledgers.
